title: parse_sapling_address: IncorrectNetwork error arm is dead code; network check only happens in second guard
description: parse_sapling_address maps ConversionError::IncorrectNetwork to InvalidRecipient (line ~531 tx_builder.rs). But the custom SaplingExtractor's TryFromAddress implementations (try_from_sapling and try_from_unified) never return IncorrectNetwork — they always return Ok, storing the address's network in addr_network. The zcash_address framework only emits IncorrectNetwork if the impl returns it. So the IncorrectNetwork arm in the match is unreachable dead code. Network validation is actually performed by the manual check at lines 542–558, which duplicates what should happen at the TryFromAddress boundary. A future contributor simplifying the code could accidentally delete either the dead arm or the live guard, silently accepting wrong-network addresses.
